headline: Alone in Algiers: The activists fighting to preserve the Hirak movement's legacy
description: 'Resisting a repressive regime (3/3). Six years after the end of the pro-democracy movement that shook the country, judicial harassment continues to target those still seeking to promote freedom of expression.

France and Germany abandon joint fighter jet program, German government says
description: Abandoning the project will be a blow to efforts by European countries to cooperate more closely on defense and to present a united front as they contend with a hostile Russia and souring ties with the United States.

headline: Talented flutist turned epidemiology engineer stands trial for joining IS with her family
description: Under her husband's influence, Camille F. moved to Syria, where she spent nine years enduring war and loss before being repatriated with her five children in 2022. She is standing trial in Paris starting Monday.

headline: Nintendo fined €35 million in France over defect in Switch Joy-Con controllers
description: Although the company became aware of a defect in its controllers as early as 2018, it waited until 2020 to communicate about the issue and until 2023 to officially offer a solution. France's competition, consumer and fraud watchdog described this as a 'misleading commercial practice.'

headline: 'I am a libertarian and belong to no party': The octogenarian, his anarchist friends and the mystery of a bomb at McDonald's
description: In July 2025, an explosive device and a torn Israeli flag were left in front of a McDonald's in France. After a 10-month investigation, an 85-year-old former press photographer, who claims to be outraged by the war in Gaza, emerged as the main suspect, while a possible accomplice died by suicide on May 7, just before an anti-terrorism operation.

description: Projects such as Le Large, built on a historic Renault factory site on Seguin island, or at the Maison du Peuple in Paris's northwestern suburbs, illustrate how major corporations are taking over former industrial sites.

headline: Pope Leo XIV quotes Gandalf, highlighting Tolkien's close ties with Catholicism
description: A Lord of the Rings reference in the last papal encyclical has sparked a great deal of commentary. While it can be read as a response to the way Silicon Valley libertarians have appropriated Tolkien's writing, it also highlights the English author's religious convictions.

headline: Farida Rustamova: 'Putin is at an impasse, unable to win or abandon this war'
description: Despite the Russian president's refusal to meet with his Ukrainian counterpart, exiled Russian political scientist Farida Rustamova sees signs that the Russian leader may be open to negotiations, which she shares in an interview.
